description: OAuth 2.0 / OIDC chain attacks beyond the base oauth.json: mix-up (RFC 6749 §10.10), audience confusion, JWKS swap via kid header injection, PKCE downgrade, redirect_uri parser quirks. References: PortSwigger OAuth labs, draft-ietf-oauth-security-topics.

oauth_mixup_attack.description: Mix-up: attacker tricks client into sending code/token to attacker AS, then attacker forwards to legitimate AS. Detected via iss parameter mismatch or absent iss in token response.

probes.description: Send token request with iss claim pointing at attacker AS. Vulnerable client doesn't validate iss against the AS metadata it used.

oauth_audience_confusion.description: Send token issued for Audience A to Resource Server B. Vulnerable RS doesn't validate aud claim and accepts any signed token from the same IdP.

probes.description: Use a token minted for a different audience. Operator captures victim token from a sister app, replays here.

jwks_kid_swap.description: JWT signature verification via JWKS — kid header tells verifier which key to use. If verifier fetches JWKS from URL in kid, attacker hosts own JWKS with matching kid.

probes.description: Kid header injection — verifier fetches JWKS from attacker URL
Kid path-traversal — read alternative JWKS from disk / unintended endpoint

pkce_downgrade.description: PKCE downgrade — request /authorize without code_challenge. Vulnerable AS issues code anyway. Then exchange without code_verifier.

probes.description: Authorize-without-PKCE — vulnerable AS issues code that can be exchanged without verifier

redirect_uri_parser_quirks.description: redirect_uri parser bypass — fragment, userinfo, path-segment confusion, fake-port, urlencoded path. Many AS implementations are looser than RFC requires.

probes.description: Userinfo trick — many parsers see authority=legit.example.com but actual host=attacker.tld
Substring-match bypass — redirect_uri startsWith / contains check
Fragment-confusion — parser splits at # but UA sends whole string
Double-decode bypass — server decodes %2F twice, attacker tail wins
